report.title: guinea-pig — LLM vulnerability ground truth (context-stripped)
report.generated_by: derived from reports/vulnerabilities.json + llm/ context-stripping pass
report.purpose: Answer key for evaluating LLM-based vulnerability detection over the llm/ directory. Files there are renamed to opaque UUIDs and stripped of comments, CWE references and tell-tale identifier names so an LLM cannot infer the planted weakness from context — only from the code itself.
report.layout: llm/<language>/<uuid>.<ext>  — one file per source fixture; a file may carry several weaknesses.
report.scope: Positives only (intentionally-weak fixtures). Each `file` is expected to be flagged for every CWE listed under `weaknesses`. The `source` field points back to the original annotated fixture and must NOT be shown to the model under test.

fields.file: context-stripped fixture under llm/ that the scanner/LLM sees
fields.source: original annotated fixture (held back from the model — used for scoring/traceability)
fields.weaknesses[].expected_cwe: ground-truth CWE the LLM should report for this file
fields.weaknesses[].scanner_types: scanner family/families that normally detect it

report.note: Regenerate from reports/vulnerabilities.json if fixtures change.

cwe.name: Buffer Copy without Checking Size of Input (Classic Buffer Overflow)

weaknesses.title: CWE-120: Buffer Copy without Checking Size of Input ('Classic Buffer Overflow')
weaknesses.description: Copies an unbounded argv string into a fixed-size stack buffer.

cwe.name: Buffer Copy without Checking Size of Input (Classic Buffer Overflow)

weaknesses.title: CWE-120: Buffer Copy without Checking Size of Input ('Classic Buffer Overflow')
weaknesses.description: Copies attacker-controlled argv data into a fixed-size stack buffer without bounds checking.

cwe.name: Stack-based Buffer Overflow

weaknesses.title: CWE-121: Stack-based Buffer Overflow
weaknesses.description: Formats attacker-controlled input into a small stack buffer with no size limit.

cwe.name: Improperly Controlled Modification of Object Prototype Attributes (Prototype Pollution)

weaknesses.title: CWE-1321: Improperly Controlled Modification of Object Prototype Attributes ('Prototype Pollution')
weaknesses.description: A recursive merge copies attacker-controlled keys (including __proto__) into a target object.
weaknesses.snippet: target[key] = merge(target[key], source[key]) without key filtering

cwe.name: Use of Externally-Controlled Format String

weaknesses.title: CWE-134: Use of Externally-Controlled Format String
weaknesses.description: User-controlled argv is passed directly as the format string.

cwe.name: Use of Externally-Controlled Format String

weaknesses.title: CWE-134: Use of Externally-Controlled Format String
weaknesses.description: Passes attacker-controlled argv directly as the printf format string.

cwe.name: Integer Overflow or Wraparound

weaknesses.title: CWE-190: Integer Overflow or Wraparound
weaknesses.description: A multiplication that can wrap around is used as the allocation size.

cwe.name: Integer Overflow or Wraparound

weaknesses.title: CWE-190: Integer Overflow or Wraparound
weaknesses.description: Computes an allocation size by multiplication that can wrap, under-allocating the buffer.

cwe.name: Exposure of Sensitive Information to an Unauthorized Actor

weaknesses.title: CWE-200: Exposure of Sensitive Information to an Unauthorized Actor
weaknesses.description: S3 bucket ACL is set to public-read, exposing stored objects to anyone on the internet.

cwe.name: Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory (Path Traversal)

weaknesses.title: CWE-22: Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory ('Path Traversal')
weaknesses.description: User-controlled filename is combined with a base directory and read without validation.

cwe.name: Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory (Path Traversal)

weaknesses.title: CWE-22: Path Traversal
weaknesses.description: A user-supplied filename is used to build a filesystem path without validation.

weaknesses.title: CWE-22: Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory ('Path Traversal')
weaknesses.description: Untrusted request input is used as a file path without validation, allowing directory traversal.

cwe.name: Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory (Path Traversal)

weaknesses.title: CWE-22: Path Traversal
weaknesses.description: A file path is built from request input without validation, allowing '../' escapes.

cwe.name: Use of Inherently Dangerous Function

weaknesses.title: CWE-242: Use of Inherently Dangerous Function
weaknesses.description: gets() cannot limit input length and always risks a buffer overflow.

weaknesses.title: CWE-250: Execution with Unnecessary Privileges

cwe.name: Time-of-check Time-of-use (TOCTOU) Race Condition

weaknesses.title: CWE-367: Time-of-check Time-of-use (TOCTOU) Race Condition
weaknesses.description: Checks access permission on a path, then opens it for writing, allowing a swap in between.
